title: You Can't Always Get What You Want

earnings.assumptions: The estimate uses a conservative artist-side range below Gimme Shelter and models gross track, label, publishing, and writer lanes from the song's legacy catalog and sync potential.

ownership.master_owner: ABKCO-controlled Rolling Stones 1960s master in current digital catalog availability.
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer-side value is modeled separately because Jagger/Richards songwriting participation is central to the composition.
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No song-specific catalog sale adjustment is modeled.
ownership.notes: The track is included to give Let It Bleed more than one modeled song support point without treating the estimate as a disclosed royalty statement.

meaning_summary: Its choir-led opening, long build, and repeated use in film, television, and public events keep it active as a classic-rock catalog song.
revenue_drivers: Catalog streaming and classic-rock playlist inclusion support recurring listening.
Sync and event reuse can lift annual demand beyond baseline streaming.
Recognition across generations keeps the song commercially useful decades after release.
